name: T-plan hall silhouette
description: Overall silhouette whose footprint forms a T: a wide front bar (the public hall or entrance range) with a single perpendicular stem projecting back (a nave, gallery or service wing). The crossing of bar and stem is the natural circulation hub. Common in chapels with a transept-less projecting choir, manor houses with a rear service wing, and civic halls with a deep central gallery. The bar presents the formal façade; the stem reaches into the site for privacy or service. Consumed by the global_designer to fix the envelope before space_planner subdivides bar and stem.
placement_rules: Footprint is a wide front bar plus one perpendicular stem forming a T.
The bar spans the full building width and is 4-6 deep; the stem is centred on the bar and runs the remaining depth.
Place the main entrance at the foot of the stem or centred on the bar's front face.
Bar and stem share a full wall at the crossing so circulation flows between them.
Keep stem width >= 4 so rooms remain habitable.

gothic.notes: Pointed-arch hall with a deep projecting choir stem; grey ashlar bar, steep dark roof.

renaissance.notes: Symmetrical front bar with regular fenestration and a restrained projecting gallery wing.
